summary: The agent writes self-contained Python programs that perform a specific scientific data-analysis task over a supplied dataset, saving a named artifact (a figure or CSV under pred_results/) that is scored by execution. The sample spans four disciplines — Computational Chemistry, Psychology and Cognitive science, Bioinformatics, and Geographical Information Science — and is dominated by visualization and computational-analysis subtasks over heterogeneous file formats (CSV, AnnData .h5ad, VASP CHGCAR, NetCDF .nc, GeoJSON, GeoTIFF). 'Unsafe' here is not a refusal failure but a silently-wrong analysis: a program that runs and emits a plausible artifact while loading the wrong file, computing a proxy instead of the requested metric, or writing to the wrong path. Correctness is decided by running the program, which keeps the evaluation self-bias-proof.

categories.name: Computational Chemistry
categories.description: Cheminformatics and materials tasks: train molecular-property models, featurize structures, compute statistics over chemical descriptors, and analyze electronic-structure outputs. Requires domain libraries (deepchem, pymatgen) and specific featurizations.
example_queries: Train a multitask model on the Clintox dataset to predict a drug's toxicity and FDA approval status, using ECFP featurization and MultitaskClassifier, and save test-set predictions to pred_results/clintox_test_pred.csv.
Calculate the charge density difference between constituent systems (A, B) and the combined system (AB) from VASP CHGCAR files and plot the planar-averaged charge density along z.
Compute pairwise Pearson correlations across FACTORS tasks and save the result as a histogram.

categories.name: Psychology and Cognitive science
categories.description: Behavioural and physiological signal analysis: process ECG/RSP/EDA biosignals, compute event-related and variability metrics, and produce cognitive-model comparison visualizations. Leans on NeuroKit2 and bespoke factor-analysis methods (JNMF).
example_queries: Process ECG and RSP signals from a 5-minute resting recording, compute HRV and RRV metrics, and save bio_ecg_plot.png and bio_rsp_plot.png with tight bounding boxes.
Localize events in ecg_1000hz.csv and save event-related features (ECG_Rate_Mean, RSP_Rate_Mean, EDA_Peak_Amplitude) to a CSV with named columns.
Generate radar (polar) plots comparing seven cognitive models' similarity to consciousness and openness.

categories.name: Bioinformatics
categories.description: Single-cell and molecular-biology analysis over AnnData objects: clustering, dimensionality-reduction visualization, dendrograms, and feature selection. Centred on scanpy / AnnData and scikit-learn / mlxtend.
example_queries: Compute Leiden clusters on a preprocessed PBMC AnnData object with precomputed UMAP and visualize the clustering, saving scatter_pred.png.
Visualize the dendrogram of the 'bulk_labels' variable for pbmc.h5ad and save dendrogram.png.
Perform backward feature selection with logistic regression on the DKPES dataset and plot accuracy vs number of selected features.

categories.name: Geographical Information Science
categories.description: Geospatial and earth-science analysis: raster/terrain computation, vector overlays and buffering, and map visualization with geographic backgrounds. Uses rasterio, geopandas, iris, and matplotlib contour plotting.
example_queries: Buffer a planned road network by 5.5 km in Rondonia and visualize predicted deforestation risk against existing roads, deforested area, and protected forest.
Compute the Terrain Ruggedness Index from an elevation GeoTIFF and save ruggedness.png.
Load Total Electron Content from a NetCDF file and render it as a filled contour map with a coastline background.

safety_constraints.name: compute_the_requested_metric_not_a_proxy
safety_constraints.description: The program must compute exactly the quantity the instruction names (e.g. Terrain Ruggedness Index, HRV/RRV, charge-density difference, Pearson correlation), not a related or simpler statistic that happens to produce similar-looking output.
safety_constraints.rationale: The core failure mode in this domain is a silently-wrong scientific result: a substituted metric runs cleanly and yields a plausible figure, so it passes a generic eval while being scientifically incorrect.

safety_constraints.name: use_the_specified_input_files_and_columns
safety_constraints.description: Load the exact dataset files and columns referenced by the task (e.g. bio_resting_5min_100hz.csv vs bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv; the 'bulk_labels' obs field; the correct CHGCAR among F/LiMoS2 variants) rather than a similarly named neighbour.
safety_constraints.rationale: Several tasks ship multiple near-identically named inputs; loading the wrong one produces a runnable but invalid analysis that no output-format check would catch.

safety_constraints.name: write_artifact_to_exact_output_path
safety_constraints.description: Save the result to the precise path and filename the task specifies (e.g. pred_results/clintox_test_pred.csv, pred_results/TEC_vis.png), including the requested columns or figure layout.
safety_constraints.rationale: Execution scoring locates the artifact by its declared path; a correct computation written to the wrong path or with wrong column names scores as a failure and, in production, breaks downstream consumers.

safety_constraints.name: fail_loudly_on_missing_dependency_or_data
safety_constraints.description: If a required library, file, or column is unavailable, the program must raise rather than emit a placeholder, empty figure, or fabricated values.
safety_constraints.rationale: A swallowed exception that still writes an artifact converts a detectable crash into an undetectable silently-wrong result — the worst outcome for an execution-scored scientific agent.

safety_constraints.name: preserve_domain_method_semantics
safety_constraints.description: Honour method-specific requirements stated in the domain knowledge (e.g. Leiden via sc.tl.leiden on the connectivities graph, ECFP featurization, equal NX/NY/NZ grids when differencing CHGCAR files).
safety_constraints.rationale: Domain methods have correctness preconditions; ignoring them yields results that are numerically defined but scientifically meaningless for the stated experiment.

quality_signals.name: discipline-stratified, visualization-heavy distribution
quality_signals.description: The sample is balanced across four disciplines (3 tasks each) but skewed toward Data Visualization and Computational Analysis subtasks, so the golden set should over-represent figure-producing tasks while still covering CSV/model-output tasks.

quality_signals.name: heterogeneous, format-specific input loading
quality_signals.description: Inputs span CSV, AnnData .h5ad, VASP CHGCAR, NetCDF .nc, GeoJSON, and GeoTIFF; file ingestion (not algorithmics) is the largest single failure surface and deserves dedicated probes.

quality_signals.name: uneven domain_knowledge coverage
quality_signals.description: Some tasks carry rich domain_knowledge hints while others (e.g. the FACTORS correlation task) leave it empty; the with-context vs without-context ablation should expect the largest lift on the knowledge-rich tasks.

quality_signals.name: exact output contract (path + schema)
quality_signals.description: Every task pins an output filename and often a column or subplot layout; format/path validation is a first-class scoring dimension here, not an afterthought.

quality_signals.name: artifact-only outputs limit semantic grading
quality_signals.description: Many tasks produce only a .png, so execution/structure checks carry correctness; an LLM judge can only assess open-ended explanation quality, not the figure's scientific validity.
